text.title: test-fixtures Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for test-fixtures as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: `scope="module"` fixtures with mutable state cause cross-test pollution. Symptom: tests pass alone, fail in suite.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Default to `function` scope until perf forces wider. Always profile before widening scope.

failure-mode.description: A `yield`-style fixture's cleanup runs in `finally`; if the test panics during teardown, cleanup raises a second exception that masks the original.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Test the teardown path separately if cleanup is complex. Keep cleanup minimal and idempotent.

failure-mode.description: `make_user()` defaults change → 200 tests' implicit assumptions silently change.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pin defaults in a single source of truth and force keyword-only overrides. Use git blame to find callers before changing factory signatures.

failure-mode.description: `db_session` fixture tied to SQLAlchemy → moving to async breaks every fixture transitively.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Keep ORM-specific fixtures in separate conftest files. Use abstract database fixtures where possible.

failure-mode.description: pytest's autouse fixtures evaluated per-test; fixture cost balloons. Mark expensive fixtures `scope="session"` only when truly read-only.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Profile with `pytest --durations=20`. Only widen scope after confirmation of immutability.

failure-mode.description: `faker.email()` produces different values each run → snapshot tests flake. Seed faker once per run (`Faker.seed(42)`).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use sequenced determinism for snapshot-critical fields. Faker is acceptable for non-critical fields only.

failure-mode.description: Pytest reverses fixture finalizers; cross-fixture dependencies that share resources need explicit ordering or `request.addfinalizer`.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Keep fixtures independent. If order matters, it's a sign the fixtures are too coupled.

failure-mode.description: pytest-xdist: Module-scope fixtures rebuild per worker; agents assume "session" means cross-process.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fixtures don't share state across workers. Use a dedicated database fixture for cross-worker state.
